seo.title: Google ADK Guardrails for Legal
seo.description: Compliance guide for Google ADK agents in legal. Meet Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Legal teams using Google ADK face heightened risks due to multi-agent systems powered by Gemini models, where circular dependencies, model switching inconsistencies, and session state issues can lead to hallucinated case citations or breaches of attorney-client privilege. Compliance guardrails are essential to prevent malpractice suits, bar sanctions, and case dismissals as seen in the Mata v. Avianca precedent. Vex provides runtime checks via before_agent_callback to enforce verifiable outputs and protect sensitive client data.

complianceRequirements.description: All client confidential information must remain protected and not be disclosed or used in AI processing without explicit safeguards.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans agent inputs/outputs for PII and privileged data, blocking transmission to Gemini models and enforcing data isolation.

complianceRequirements.description: AI-generated legal advice must be reviewed by a licensed attorney and cannot substitute for professional judgment.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex flags AI-generated content requiring human review and logs attribution for ethics compliance audits.

complianceRequirements.description: All citations must reference real, verifiable cases; hallucinated citations are grounds for sanctions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates case citations against legal databases before agent execution, rejecting unverified references.

complianceRequirements.description: AI tools in discovery must preserve chain of custody and prevent data leakage across sessions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex monitors session state to prevent corruption and ensures tool responses match strict eDiscovery formats.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated case citations
industryRisks.description: Agents generate fake case law due to model switching or circular delegation.
industryRisks.example: Mata v. Avianca: Court sanctioned lawyers for AI-fabricated citations, leading to case dismissal.

industryRisks.name: Privilege data leakage
industryRisks.description: Multi-agent handoffs expose client secrets via inconsistent tool formats or state corruption.
industryRisks.example: Confidential strategy shared between research and drafting agents without isolation.

industryRisks.name: Unreviewed AI advice
industryRisks.description: Gemini output inconsistencies bypass human review, violating ethics rules.
industryRisks.example: Agent recommends invalid plea deal without attorney verification.

industryRisks.name: eDiscovery chain breakage
industryRisks.description: Session state corruption alters document metadata across long conversations.
industryRisks.example: Tampered timestamps lead to spoliation claims and sanctions.

implementation.title: Validate Case Citations Pre-Agent Execution

implementation.description: Monitor multi-agent delegation to block loops and enforce session isolation.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Google ADK in Legal: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A mid-sized law firm deploys Google ADK for civil litigation support. ResearchAgent (Gemini 2.0 Flash) gathers case law, AnalysisAgent summarizes precedents, and DraftAgent generates motions. In a high-stakes contract dispute, the research agent hallucinates a non-existent 9th Circuit case due to model switching mid-session. AnalysisAgent incorporates it without verification, creating circular references back to ResearchAgent for 'clarification.' The corrupted session state causes DraftAgent to output a motion with fabricated citations, which junior associates file without review, violating court standards.

Without guardrails, client confidential settlement terms leak across agents during a long discovery conversation, breaching privilege. The hallucinated citations trigger opposing counsel's AI-detection tools, leading to sanctions motions citing Mata v. Avianca.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: With Vex integrated as before_agent_callback, the privilege check blocks the settlement data from crossing agents. Citation validation rejects the fake 9th Circuit case pre-execution, forcing human researcher intervention. Circular dependency detection halts the looping delegation, preserving session integrity. The motion drafts include Vex audit logs proving human review of all AI outputs, satisfying bar ethics rules. The firm avoids sanctions, maintains client trust, and uses Vex reports for malpractice insurance defense.

cta.heading: Comply with Legal Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex for Google ADK today to protect your firm from AI compliance failures - pip install vex-sdk.
